intro.title: Linear Combinations
paragraphs: A linear combination is just the skills you already have, used together:
Here is 2A + B: scale A by 2, connect head-to-tail, then draw the result.

interstitials.heading: You can also just use the components

segments.value: A linear combination combines two things you already know: scalar multiplication and vector addition (and subtraction).
So instead of drawing, you can use the same component logic: multiply each vector's components by its scalar, then add the matching components.

questions.prompt: Build 2A + B on the grid, then fill in the vector’s components below.
questions.hint: Scale A by 2 to get ⟨4, 2⟩, then connect B = ⟨1, 2⟩ onto its head. The result runs from the origin to where B ends.
questions.explanation: 2A + B = 2·⟨2, 1⟩ + ⟨1, 2⟩ = ⟨4, 2⟩ + ⟨1, 2⟩ = ⟨5, 4⟩. Scaling, connecting head-to-tail, and drawing the result is all a linear combination is.

questions.prompt: Build A + 3B on the grid, then fill in the vector’s components below.
questions.hint: Keep A = ⟨2, 1⟩ as it is, scale B by 3 to get ⟨3, 6⟩, then connect them head-to-tail.

questions.prompt: What is 2A − B? Fill in the vector’s components below. (Subtracting B is the same as adding −B.)
questions.hint: 2A − B = 2A + (−B). Scale A by 2 and scale B by −1 (drag B's head through the origin so it points the opposite way), then connect them.

questions.prompt: What is −A + 2B? Fill in the vector’s components below.
questions.hint: Scale A by −1 (drag its head through the origin) and scale B by 2, then connect them head-to-tail.

questions.prompt: Scale A, connect B head-to-tail, then scale B until the path reaches the highlighted point. Then fill in the scalars c and d that make c·A + d·B land on it.
questions.hint: Scale A and B until the path lands on ⟨4, 5⟩. The scale factor you used on A is c, and the one on B is d.

questions.prompt: Scale A, connect B head-to-tail, then scale B until the path reaches the highlighted point. Then fill in the scalars c and d.
questions.hint: Scale A and B until the path lands on ⟨6, 6⟩. The scale factor on A is c, and the one on B is d.
